article_title: Lilly Bets Up to $2.25 Billion on Profluent's AI-Designed Recombinases, Pushing Gene Editing Beyond CRISPR Into Kilobase-Scale Territory

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: Lilly Bets Up to $2.25 Billion on Profluent's AI-Designed Recombinases, Pushing Gene Editing Beyond CRISPR Into Kilobase-Scale Territory
content_preview.summary: Eli Lilly's deal with Bezos-backed Profluent funds AI-designed recombinases that aim to insert entire genes, going beyond CRISPR's localized cuts.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Eli Lilly has signed a strategic research collaboration with AI biotech Profluent worth up to $2.25 billion in milestone payments, betting that artificial intelligence can design a class of gene-editing enzymes capable of inserting entire genes — a feat that current CRISPR-based tools cannot reliably achieve. The deal, announced on April 28, 2026, focuses on AI-designed site-specific recombinases and marks Lilly's second recombinase pact in four months.

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid biotech reporting (766 words). Substantive deal facts verified across STAT and Yahoo Finance (live), with the Seamless background fully verified verbatim from BioPharma Dive.

source_verification.https://www.statnews.com/2026/04/28/eli-: source-0.html (datePublished 2026-04-28). Verified for: $2.25 billion in milestones, Profluent + Lilly partnership, 'sparse on details' framing (number of programs, diseases, upfront unspecified), Lilly's broader push 'flush with record revenues from its obesity and diabetes drugs', 'genetic medicine center in Boston', 'bought up a series of gene editing or gene therapy companies'. NOT in STAT: tiered royalties, exclusive license language, multiple genomic targets.
source_verification.https://www.biopharmadive.com/news/eli-l: source-1.html (article_date_original 2026-01-28). Verified verbatim: 'over $1.12 billion in total payouts', Germany-based Seamless Therapeutics, 'launched in 2023 with $12.5 million in seed funding led by Wellington Management and Forbion', CEO Albert Seymour, 'is a validation of our gene editing platform and its broad disease-modifying potential' quote, 'precisely pinpoint and modify specific areas of the genome' quote, hearing-loss focus.
source_verification.https://finance.yahoo.com/sectors/health: source-2.html — SNAPSHOT FAILED (effectively): the captured page is a privacy/cookie-consent gate ('Le tue scelte relative alla privacy'), only 110 lines, with no article content. Per skill protocol, fell back to live WebFetch as last resort. WebFetch confirmed: 'Profluent could earn up to $2.25 billion in milestone payments, as well as royalties on future sales' (royalties — but NOT 'tiered'); 'Lilly will have exclusive rights to further develop selected enzymes and bring any resulting medicines to market' (exclusive rights — close paraphrase of 'exclusive license'); 'Profluent will use its artificial intelligence technology to create custom enzymes designed to make precise changes to DNA' (NO mention of 'multiple genomic targets'); upfront payment + research funding mentioned, with 'further financial details were not disclosed'.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Substantive deal facts verified. Three minor paraphrasing concerns: (1) article says 'tiered royalties on net sales' attributed to Yahoo Finance, but Yahoo says only 'royalties on future sales' — the 'tiered' specifier is not in the source; (2) article says 'multiple genomic targets' attributed to Yahoo Finance, but Yahoo says only 'create custom enzymes designed to make precise changes to DNA' — 'multiple genomic targets' is editorial extrapolation; (3) 'exclusive license' is close paraphrase of Yahoo's 'exclusive rights' — defensible.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, with appropriate flagging of safety/clinical uncertainties.
editor_notes.originality: Profluent + Lilly deal not previously covered. The Seamless/January 2026 backstory provides useful context.
concerns: Yahoo Finance snapshot is a privacy gate (effective failure). Used WebFetch fallback per skill protocol — most claims verifiable, with the two minor paraphrasing notes above.
Article uses finance.yahoo.com URL (allowlist has yahoofinance.com). Established precedent in published articles; non-blocking but worth aligning the allowlist.
recommendations: Optional: tighten the two attributions to Yahoo Finance — 'tiered royalties on net sales' could be replaced with 'royalties on future sales' (closer to source); 'multiple genomic targets' could be removed or rephrased to match the source's general 'precise changes to DNA' phrasing.
Consider adding finance.yahoo.com to the source allowlist (currently only yahoofinance.com is listed) — there is established usage in published articles.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Substantive deal facts verified ($2.25B, AI-designed recombinases, Lilly's broader gene editing push, Seamless backstory). Yahoo snapshot privacy-gate problem worked around via WebFetch fallback. Minor paraphrasing concerns flagged in recommendations but not blocking.
